verdict.summary: rust-craft, Apollo, and no skill each passed the same 3 of 4 tasks. This pilot does not demonstrate added capability from rust-craft.

task_findings.finding: All final code states were identical after excluding EVAL_REPORT.md.
All configurations produced valid owned, joinable task lifecycles.
Every solution used a non-Send boxed future without documenting that executor limitation in the public API. All other critical assertions passed.
All wrappers passed the boundary tests and independent Send/Sync trait probes.

strengths: Matched task fixtures and prompts across configurations.
Fresh executor per trial and isolated final states.
Frozen skill, fixture, and eval snapshots recorded by hash.
Configuration-blind grading with evidence per assertion.
limitations: One run per task and configuration is not a reliability estimate.
The harness exposes global skills, so without_skill relies on an explicit prohibition rather than physical removal.
Executor traces, token counts, and effective runtime model identity are unavailable from the collaboration harness.
TASK.md centralizes many decisive constraints, reducing the need for rust-craft's project-contract discovery workflow.
Three tasks were saturated; the fourth failed identically across configurations.

disposition.current_tasks: Retain as regression candidates, not evidence of differential capability.
disposition.next_iteration: Design harder tasks whose necessary Rust constraints are discoverable in project files but not restated in the prompt; freeze them before execution and keep the same three-way blind comparison.
